content.problemStatement:
There are NNN lily pads, arranged in a line. They are numbered 111 to NNN from left to right.

A frog is initially standing on lily pad NNN. The frog loves to jump, so it will perform a sequence of (N‚àí1)(N-1)(N‚àí1) jumps with alternating directions as follows:


First, jump to the lily pad N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 positions to the left of its current position.
Next, jump to the lily pad N‚àí2N-2N‚àí2 positions to the right of its current position.
Next, jump to the lily pad N‚àí3N-3N‚àí3 positions to the left of its current position.

‚ãÆ\vdots‚ãÆ
More formally, in the iii-th jump (1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí1(1 \le i \le N-1(1‚â§i‚â§N‚àí1),


If iii is odd, the frog will jump to the lily pad N‚àíiN-iN‚àíi positions to the left of its current position.

That is, if its current position is XXX, its new position will be X‚àí(N‚àíi)X - (N-i)X‚àí(N‚àíi).
If iii is even, the frog will jump to the lily pad N‚àíiN-iN‚àíi positions to the right of its current position.

That is, if its current position is XXX, its new position will be X+(N‚àíi)X + (N-i)X+(N‚àíi).
It can be proved that all of the frog's jumps are valid - that is, it will always be on one of the lily pads 111 to NNN.

You're given NNN. Can you find the number of the final lily pad the frog will end up at?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input, containing a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the answer: the final position of the frog.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are two lily pads, numbered 111 and 222. The frog is on lily pad 222.
There will be N‚àí1=1N-1 = 1N‚àí1=1 move made, in which the frog will jump 111 step left and hence end up on lily pad 111.
Test case 222: There are three lily pads, numbered 1,2,1,2,1,2, and 333. The frog is on lily pad 333.
There will be N‚àí1=2N-1 = 2N‚àí1=2 jumps made, as follows:

First jump: jump 222 positions left. The frog is now on lily pad 3‚àí2=13-2=13‚àí2=1.
Second jump: jump 111 position right. The frog is now on lily pad 1+1=21+1=21+1=2.

So, in the end, the frog is on lily pad 222.
Test case 333: The frog is on lily pad N=5N = 5N=5. Four jumps will be made, as follows:

First jump: 444 positions left. The frog is now on lily pad 5‚àí4=15-4=15‚àí4=1.
Second jump: 333 positions right. The frog is now on lily pad 1+3=41+3=41+3=4.
Third jump: 222 positions left. The frog is now on lily pad 4‚àí2=24-2=24‚àí2=2.
Fourth jump: 111 positions right. The frog is now on lily pad 2+1=32+1=32+1=3.

So, the final answer is 333.
